title: Te Whakatau Taurangi o Te Awa Mangakōtukutuku / Algebraic Modelling of the Mangakōtukutuku Stream
subject: Mathematics
culturalContext: Students will investigate water quality and flow patterns of Te Awa Mangakōtukutuku, the stream that flows through our college lands and holds significance to Ngāti Kahungunu as a traditional food gathering site and boundary marker. The stream's health reflects our community's kaitiakitanga practices.
learningObjectives: Create linear and quadratic functions to model water quality data collected from Te Awa Mangakōtukutuku
Calculate rates of change in water pH and turbidity levels using algebraic methods
Analyze mathematical models to predict seasonal variations in stream health
Propose evidence-based restoration strategies using mathematical justification

activities.title: Whakapapa o Te Awa - Stream Story Mapping
activities.description: Students work with kaumātua to map the historical path and significance of Te Awa Mangakōtukutuku, identifying key measurement points and creating coordinate systems based on traditional landmarks

activities.title: Pūtaiao Ine - Scientific Data Collection
activities.description: Field trip to collect water samples at 5 designated points along the stream, measuring pH, turbidity, and flow rate using digital sensors and traditional observation methods

activities.title: Whakatau Taurangi - Algebraic Modelling
activities.description: Students create linear regression models for pH levels and quadratic functions for seasonal flow rates using graphing calculators and spreadsheet software, comparing modern data with historical records

activities.title: Te Arotakenga Tauanga - Statistical Analysis
activities.description: Calculate rates of change between measurement points, determine correlation coefficients for water quality indicators, and identify trends using statistical measures

activities.title: Te Whakatau Kaitiaki - Guardianship Solutions
activities.description: Groups develop mathematically justified proposals for stream restoration, presenting cost-benefit analyses and projected improvement timelines to community representatives

resources: Mangakōtukutuku Stream historical water quality data from Hawke's Bay Regional Council
Digital pH meters and turbidity tubes from NZ Marine Studies Centre
Kaumātua Rangi Smith (Ngāti Kahungunu environmental knowledge holder)
Desmos graphing calculator and Google Sheets for data analysis

tasks: Create a mathematical model predicting water quality changes over 12 months
Write a technical report justifying restoration recommendations with mathematical evidence
Present findings to community panel using appropriate mathematical language and cultural protocols

nzcAlignment: Mathematics and Statistics Level 5: Equations and expressions
Mathematics and Statistics Level 5: Patterns and relationships
Science Level 5: Planet Earth and Beyond
Social Sciences Level 5: Economic activities and sustainability
